title: Haggai 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Haggai
paragraphs: 1 In year two of King Darius, on day one of month six, the LORD spoke through Haggai the prophet. He spoke to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest. He said:
2 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: “These people say, ‘The time has not come to rebuild the LORD’s house.’”
3 Then the LORD spoke through Haggai the prophet again:
4 “Is it right for you to live in nice wooden houses, while this house is broken down?”
5 Now this is what the LORD of Hosts says: “Think carefully about how you live.
6 You plant much but harvest little. You eat but are never full. You drink but are never satisfied. You wear clothes but are never warm. You earn money, but it falls through a bag full of holes.”
7 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: “Think carefully about how you live.
8 Go up into the hills, bring down wood, and build the house. Then I will be pleased with it and be honored,” says the LORD.

9 “You hoped for much, but look, it came to little. What you brought home, I blew away. Why? declares the LORD of Hosts. Because My house still lies broken, while each of you cares only for his own house.
10 So because of you the sky has held back its dew. The ground has held back its crops.
11 I called a drought on the fields and on the hills. It came on the grain, new wine, and oil. It came on all the ground gives, on people and animals, and on all the work of your hands.”

12 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel and Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, with all the rest of the people, obeyed the voice of the LORD their God. They obeyed the words of Haggai the prophet, because the LORD their God had sent him. So the people feared the LORD.
13 Then Haggai, the messenger of the LORD, gave the LORD’s message to the people: “I am with you,” declares the LORD.

14 So the LORD moved the heart of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah. He moved the heart of Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest. He also moved the heart of all the rest of the people. They came and began work on the house of the LORD of Hosts, their God,
15 on day twenty-four of month six, in year two of King Darius.

title: Haggai 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Haggai
paragraphs: 1 On the twenty-first day of the seventh month, the word of the LORD came through Haggai the prophet:
2 “Speak to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah, and to Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest, and to the people who are left. Ask them,
3 ‘Who is still here who saw this house in the glory it had before? How does it look to you now? Does it not look like nothing next to that?’

4 But now be strong, Zerubbabel, declares the LORD. Be strong, Joshua son of Jehozadak, the high priest. Be strong, all you people of the land, declares the LORD. Work! For I am with you, declares the LORD of Hosts.
5 This is the promise I made to you when you came out of Egypt. My Spirit stays with you. Do not be afraid.”
6 For this is what the LORD of Hosts says: “In a little while, once more, I will shake the sky and the earth, the sea and the dry land.
7 I will shake all the nations. They will come with all their rich gifts, and I will fill this house with glory, says the LORD of Hosts.
8 The silver is Mine, and the gold is Mine, declares the LORD of Hosts.
9 The later glory of this house will be greater than the first, says the LORD of Hosts. And in this place I will give peace, declares the LORD of Hosts.”
10 On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of the LORD came to Haggai the prophet:
11 “This is what the LORD of Hosts says: ‘Ask the priests for a decision from the law.
12 If a man carries holy meat in a fold of his clothes, and it touches bread, stew, wine, oil, or any other food, does that food become holy?’” “No,” the priests replied.
13 So Haggai asked, “If a man is unclean from touching a dead body and then touches any of these, does it become unclean?” “Yes, it becomes unclean,” the priests answered.
14 Then Haggai said, “So it is with this people and this nation before Me, declares the LORD. So it is with every work of their hands; what they offer there is unclean.
15 Now think carefully from this day on: Before one stone was placed on another in the temple of the LORD,
16 from that time, when someone came hoping for twenty ephahs of grain, there were only ten. When someone came to the winepress for fifty baths, there were only twenty.
17 I struck you—all the work of your hands—with dry rot, mildew, and hail. But you did not turn to Me, declares the LORD.
18 Think carefully from this day on—from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, the day the foundation of the LORD’s temple was laid—think carefully:
19 Is there still seed in the barn? The vine, the fig, the pomegranate, and the olive tree have not yet given fruit. But from this day on, I will bless you.”
20 A second time that day, on the twenty-fourth day of the month, the word of the LORD came to Haggai:
21 “Tell Zerubbabel governor of Judah that I am about to shake the heavens and the earth.
22 I will tip over royal thrones and break the power of the kingdoms of the nations. I will tip over chariots and their riders. Horses and their riders will fall, each by the sword of his brother.

23 On that day, declares the LORD of Hosts, I will take you, My servant, Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel, declares the LORD. I will make you like My signet ring, for I have chosen you, declares the LORD of Hosts.”
